explanation: The Sigma rule detects when wuauclt.exe is executed without command line flags. This command represents a true positive as it matches the rule's condition of an empty or missing command line.
Adding extra spaces at the end of the command line does not change the behavior and still represents a valid empty command line, matching the Sigma rule.
Enclosing the executable in quotes is a safe mutation technique that does not alter the behavior and still matches the rule's detection criteria.
Changing the case of the executable name is allowed on Windows and still matches the rule's detection criteria for wuauclt.exe.
Using the full path to the executable is a valid mutation that still represents an empty command line and matches the Sigma rule.
